subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, UN PDNA/OCHA, V-Dem, Freedom House, RSF, TI) + international press.

Acute macro-financial distress (inflation ~33%, a 44% 2023 kwacha devaluation with a steep parallel premium, an IMF ECF that lapsed in May 2025, external debt distress at ~88% of GDP), against a genuine democracy that delivered a peaceful Sept 2025 turnover.

Standout features: a comparatively free and competitive democracy with relatively strong courts, against severe macro distress and extreme climate exposure (Cyclone Freddy 2023); the drag is the economy and disasters, not repression.

economic.notes: In external debt distress with high inflation, a lapsed IMF program, and chronic forex shortages.

fiscal_state.notes: In external debt distress, public debt ~88% of GDP and interest ~7% of GDP; the ECF terminated May 2025 with restructuring unfinished. (IMF; AFRODAD)

monetary_independence.notes: The Reserve Bank has nominal autonomy but persistent forex scarcity and political pressure constrain effective policy. (IMF; press)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The kwacha is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: ~33% y/y, food- and forex-driven, persistently high through 2024-25. (IMF; Al Jazeera)

banking_stability.notes: Broadly solvent but operating amid severe forex shortages and high sovereign-debt exposure. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Landlocked, narrow export base (tobacco-dominant), chronic forex/fuel shortages, highly aid-dependent. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: Comparatively strong courts and a relatively open press, against thin administration.

rule_of_law.notes: A relatively independent judiciary (the 2020 election annulment) but persistent corruption (CPI ~34/100) - comparatively the region's stronger. (TI; Freedom House)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Thin, patronage-affected, aid-dependent, with weak revenue mobilisation. (IMF; World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: A relatively open media environment by regional standards, with episodic harassment. (RSF; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: A competitive democracy with a peaceful 2025 handover, strained by economic hardship.

political_stability.notes: A competitive democracy with a peaceful Sept 2025 handover, but fragile from severe economic stress. (Al Jazeera; Chatham House)

civil_liberties.notes: Among the freer states in the region - 'Partly Free' with real rights, tempered by violence against women. (Freedom House; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Hosts refugees (Dzaleka) under an encampment policy with movement/work restrictions. (UNHCR; HRW)

social_cohesion.notes: Regional/ethnic political cleavages but broadly peaceful; cost-of-living strain dominates. (Freedom House)

demographics.notes: Young and fast-growing; potential dividend offset by very low human capital and poverty. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A stable, Western/multilateral-aligned, conflict-free partner with de-facto forex rationing.

alliance_reliability.notes: A stable, donor-reliant, non-belligerent partner. (World Bank; inference)

conflict_involvement.notes: No armed conflict or insurgency - internally and externally peaceful. (ACLED)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not sanctioned, but acute forex rationing functions as de-facto capital/import control. (IMF; press)

physical_practical.notes: Extreme cyclone/flood/drought exposure with a weak, donor-dependent health system.

climate.notes: ND-GAIN bottom-25 - Cyclone Freddy 2023 devastation, drought-flood whiplash, rain-fed maize, very low readiness. (ND-GAIN; Germanwatch CRI)

healthcare.notes: Weak, donor-dependent, with severe workforce shortages and recurrent cholera. (WHO; HRW)

infrastructure.notes: Low electrification, poor roads, chronic fuel shortages, and landlocked corridor dependence. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate crime, with episodic hardship-driven unrest but no systemic insecurity. (OSAC)

disaster_insurance.notes: Negligible penetration against high cyclone/flood/drought exposure. (inference)

summary: One of the world's poorest, most aid- and tobacco-dependent economies, now in acute macro-financial distress: ~33% inflation, a kwacha devalued 44% in 2023 that still trades at a steep parallel-market premium, an IMF ECF that lapsed in May 2025, and external debt distress with public debt near 88% of GDP. Politically it remains a genuine if fragile democracy - the September 2025 election produced a peaceful turnover amid cost-of-living anger - and scores comparatively well on rule of law and civil liberties for the region. Its sharpest non-economic drag is climate exposure, underscored by Cyclone Freddy's 2023 devastation. The net picture is negative, weighed down by the economy and disaster risk rather than repression or conflict.
